name: Santuario Virgen de las Lagunas y Presa Romana de Muel — circular desde Alfamén
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alfamén traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including areas with notable elevation changes and open countryside. The region offers a mix of agricultural lands and natural features, providing diverse backdrops for cycling. Routes often include sections with gradual climbs and descents, suitable for touring bicycles. The area's geology supports a range of paths, from smoother roads to more rugged tracks. Best touring cycling routes around Alfamén The most popular touring cycling route is Santuario Virgen de las Lagunas y Presa Romana de Muel — circular desde Alfamén, a 23.9 miles (38.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a circular path with some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puerto de Codos – Paniza loop from Longares, a difficult 47.9 miles (77.2 km) path. This route includes significant climbs, characteristic of a mountain pass area. Local touring cyclists also love the Mirador del Sur – El Fuerte loop from La Almunia de Doña Godina, a 16.0 miles (25.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Alfamén is defined by open landscapes, rolling hills, and agricultural areas, with options for both easy and challenging bike routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from beginner touring bike routes to more difficult ascents. The routes in Alfamén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alfamén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alfamén?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes in the Alfamén area, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists of all abilities.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alfamén?
A: Yes, Alfamén offers 3 easy touring cycling routes. These paths are generally flatter and shorter, ideal for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride through the agricultural lands and open countryside.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes around Alfamén?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 23 difficult routes. These often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40002219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Monument loop from La Almunia de Doña Godina&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alfamén?
A: Yes, many routes in Alfamén are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993116960/santuario-virgen-de-las-lagunas-y-presa-romana-de-muel-circular-desde-alfamen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de las Lagunas y Presa Romana de Muel — circular desde Alfamén&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate ride through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alfamén?
A: The routes around Alfamén traverse a landscape characterized by open countryside, rolling hills, and extensive agricultural lands. You'll encounter a mix of natural features and cultivated fields, with some routes offering views of notable elevation changes and geological formations.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by significant points of interest. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Muel&lt;/a&gt;, natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993116960/santuario-virgen-de-las-lagunas-y-presa-romana-de-muel-circular-desde-alfamen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de las Lagunas y Presa Romana de Muel — circular desde Alfamén&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, includes both the Sanctuary and the Roman Dam.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alfamén?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alfamén are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from smoother roads to more rugged tracks, and the opportunities to explore both open landscapes and areas with notable elevation changes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, routes that ascend into the rolling hills or pass through areas with elevation changes often provide scenic vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Sur – El Fuerte loop from La Almunia de Doña Godina&lt;/a&gt; is likely to offer good views, given its name suggesting a 'viewpoint'.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alfamén?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Alfamén is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if cycling during this season. Winters are generally cool but can be pleasant on sunny days.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, some of the more difficult routes venture into areas with significant climbs, characteristic of mountain pass regions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Codos – Paniza loop from Longares&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, featuring substantial elevation gain as it traverses a mountain pass area.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes around Alfamén typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Sur – El Fuerte loop from La Almunia de Doña Godina&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes, while longer, more challenging tours such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Codos – Paniza loop from Longares&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there routes that explore the local wine region?
A: While not explicitly detailed, the region around Alfamén is known for its agricultural lands, including vineyards. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carinena – ⭐️ Church of San Pedro loop from Longares&lt;/a&gt;, which passes near Cariñena, a renowned wine-producing area, would likely offer views of vineyards and opportunities to experience the local viticulture.
tips:
Beautiful sanctuary in a very quiet place in the middle of the Cariñena countryside. If we book a guided tour in advance, the count himself will explain the very interesting history of the site.
We will be able to make a stop to eat and enjoy the terrace on days of good weather.
The Muel Roman dam is one of the most relevant hydraulic civil engineering works in the Hispano-Roman world, due to its monumentality and good state of conservation.
In Alfamén we will find a route of mural paintings that brighten up the facades of the houses and make it a most picturesque town.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Lagoons
